compound_name: Indeed, for Enamine and Bergström molecules the RMSE increased for
evidence_quote: Indeed, for Enamine and Bergström molecules the RMSE increased for MP < 100 °C for both consensus models.
Of 2262 molecules with MP < 0 values, only <10% were recognized as outside the AD using this DM.

compound_name: DMs used were unable to correctly identify and filter out molecules with
evidence_quote: The DMs used were unable to correctly identify and filter out molecules with MP < 0 and thus large errors from the data set.

compound_name: dashed lines indicate a defined drug-like region, which covers the
evidence_quote: The dashed lines indicate a defined drug-like region, which covers the MP of >90 % of drugs (Bergström) and chemical provider (Enamine) set as well as 87 % of the compounds from the PATENTS set It is interesting that the experimental accuracy depended on the MP value.

compound_name: This result was due to the absence of molecules with
evidence_quote: This result was due to the absence of molecules with MP <0 °C in the PATENTS set.

compound_name: Indeed, there were only few molecules with an
evidence_quote: Indeed, there were only few molecules with an MP <0 °C in this set and, moreover, most (all) of these molecules were likely to be experimental errors (see below the section regarding the filtering of outliers).

compound_name: Enamine set also did not have co
evidence_quote: The Enamine set also did not have compounds with MP <0 °C and a model based on this set failed to predict the whole Bradley set despite the fact that it had excellent prediction ability for its “drug like” subset (Table 3 ).

compound_name: There were about 5 and 6 % molecules with
